publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2023-10-04 00:07:43 UTC.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Talcott family papers
did.physdesc: Correspondence, diaries, legal and financial documents.
did.abstract: Journal-account book (1823) kept by George Talcott, U.S. Army Ordnance Department, while on an official surveying trip through Ohio, Kentucky, and Tennessee; thirty-six Mexican War letters; one diary of Talcott's son, Sebastian Visscher Talcott. Also, personal and business correspondence and accounts, estate settlement papers, deeds, bills, and miscellaneous papers of the related Talcott, Shearman (Sherman), Bogard, and Gibson families; fair copies of 17th and 18th century wills and inventories.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Journal-account book (1823) kept by George Talcott, U.S. Army Ordnance Department, while on an official surveying trip through Ohio, Kentucky,. and Tennessee; one letter to Talcott from Winfield Scott (1842) concerning a recently invented machine for throwing shot; thirty-six Mexican War letters, chiefly to Talcott at Washington, D.C. from Benjamin Huger, Scott's chief of ordnance, concerning the capture of Vera Cruz, the march on Mexico City, the storming of Chapultepec, and the numerous difficulties encountered on this campaign, but also from Alexander Brydie Dyer, describing the action taken to quell the Taos uprising and other events, and from Josiah Gorgas, George Douglas Ramsay, and other officers; one diary (1841-1842) of Talcott's son, Sebastian Visscher Talcott, kept during the official survey of the Northeastern boundary of the United States. Also, personal and business correspondence and accounts, estate settlement papers, deeds, bills, and miscellaneous papers of the related Talcott, Shearman (Sherman), Bogard, and Gibson families; fair copies of 17th and 18th century wills and inventories. Places represented include Braintree, Essex County, England; Glastonbury, Wethersfield, Connecticut; Albany, Canandaigua, New York City, Rochester, and Utica, New York; and Danville, Illinois.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Olivia S. T. Pruyn, Collector. Talcott Family Papers, #2087. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Legal documents
Will of John Talcott

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Extracted from the Registry of the Prerogative Court of Canterbury."

did.unittitle: Inventory of John Talcott Estate
Will of Dorothy Talcott, widow of John Talcott

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inventory of Estate

did.unittitle: Inventory of Lieut. Col. John Talcott Estate
Will of Capt. Samuel Talcott

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inventory

did.unittitle: Will of Lieut. Benjamin Talcott
Will of Jonathan Shearman

controlaccess.geogname: South Kingston, Kings County, Rhode Island

did.unittitle: Certificate- 10 shares in the US Lombard Assoc. for George Talcott.
Deed from Alexander Seymour and Sands Higginbotham to Robert Sherman for Watts Sherman's estate
Revised last will of Robert Sherman.
Robert Sherman and Samuel Thomson partnership agreement "partners in trade under firm of Sherman and Thomson."
Honor Sherman's authorization for Thomas S. Taylor to lease her real estate in Rhode Island (town of Exeter) and transact her business.
Samuel Thomson signs over to Robert Sherman his right title and interest in and to the partnership debts, full power to collect and receive debts. 2- Accounts of debts to be collected and received.
[Bond] - Samuel Thomson breaks partnership with Robert Sherman and turns over all his rights and interests in the firm.
Will of Robert Sherman, Jr. (daughter- Olivia Sherman)
Above will of Robert Sherman, Jr. Cancelled.1- deprived by Samuel Thomson of the means of willing anything to anybody.
Agreement: W. Barron Williams rents from Robert Sherman a 2-story store on Carrole St. in Rochester.
Williams receives 2,000 from R. Sherman in settlement with account in the agreement.
Account of W.P. Sherman Estate (William Pitt [?])
Bond of Robert Sherman to Willette H. Sherman the sum of 4 thousand.
Bond of R. Sherman to Willette H. Sherman the sum of nine thousand.
Bond R. Sherman to Jonathan Sherman the sum of 2,000.
Bond R. Sherman to Jonathan Sherman the sum of 2,000.
R. Sherman in account with W. Barron Williams.
Williams' promise to pay R. Sherman $414.56 after 13 months.
Letter from Hon. Lew Cass (?), Department of War, Washington D.C. to Mayor George Talcott.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "Requesting suggestions for altercation [alteration?] in military code."

did.unittitle: Williams' promise to pay Sherman 242.45 after 4 months.
Account of back debts of late firm of Sherman and Thomson.
Statement of property purchases of Robert Sherman.
Statement of indebtedness of Samuel Lizer to R. Sherman and Bob Jones (about 200 dollars.)
Elisha Cary turns over all right, title and interest in the lumber at rear of Henry Seymour's lot.
Sherriff's certificate stating sale of land (Utica) owned by Abby Jane Flandreau in favor of Robert Sherman.
Robert Sherman transfers to EBEN B. Sherman the right to collect and receive the debts and notes of his accounts for endorsing his 6 promisory notes which total 2,928.86. Also account.
Letter to daughter Olivia Sherman from Robert Sherman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: R.S. illness

did.unittitle: W.B. Williams notes due to R. Sherman
W.B. Williams notes endorsed to Robert S.
Gideon Sherman and wife give up all rights and titles to Robert Sherman's will to inherit his real estate when R.S.'s wife, Honor, dies. Gideonson of R.S. of Rhode Island.
2 Bills of R. Sherman (for nails, shoes for horse, etc.)
25 week subscription to Utica Observer R.S.
Bills of R.S. for misc. material (and recetpts)
Bill of Deborah Rowland for 32.20 to the late firm of Sherman and Thomson.
Utica E.B. Sherman informs Olivia Sherman of death of her father, Robert.
Diary kept by Sebastian Vischer Talcott, son of George Talcott of Albany. He married Olivia Sherman, daughter of Robert Sherman.
Utica Certificate giving Willett H. Sherman the letters of Administration of the Robert Sherman Estate
Surrogate Letter of Administration on the personal Estate of R.S. to W.H.S.
Inventory of R.S. estate (personal property) by W.H. Sherman
Accounts of R.S. estate.
Lara Sherman as widow of R.S. releases all claims of estate to W.H. Sherman
Map 1) disputed territory upper N.Y. lower Canada
40- Bill and receipts of R.S. estate.
Letters
Toriee Goodrich, Charles Dickinson, and Jedediah Waterman form co-partnership in a ship chancellory line.
receipts, bills, letters of attorney.
2 letters. War of 1812.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: George Talcott begs G.T. Jr. to come home to Glastenbury before the British fleet gets him.

did.unittitle: Letters from Angelica Talcott (wife) to George Talcott
Letters (inventories) by Sherman.
Letter to Capt. Talcott from J. Miller Byent (?)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter to Capt. Talcott from J. Miller Byent (?) asking Talcott for permission to have the President use his horse.

did.unittitle: Watts Sherman: accounts- receipts and expenditures, accounts of Estate
Letter to Isaac H. Bogart from George Talcott

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Subject: Burning of steamboat; boilers explode, narrow escape for George and Angelica and children

did.unittitle: Accounts for Estate in New York
Maria Sherman- one of the executors of Watts Sherman Estate
Will of Watts Sherman (New York City) Inventory of Estate
Letter to Maria Sherman from Olivia Sherman
Account of Watts Sherman Estate
2 letters to Maria Shearman from Robert (husband)
Amanda L. Sherman Account
Opinions on Will of. Watts Sherman by Judge Jonas Platt
Opinion of Joseph Kirkland.
George Talcott- US Army surveyor Diary
2 letters of Administration on the estates of Olivia and Lucy A. Sherman. Administrator: Robert Shearman
Personal property record of Watts Sherman
2 Accounts of Watts Sherman Estate
Certificate to George Talcott for degree of Master Mason
E.B. Sherman lease to James H. Hackett- for 2 years from May 1, 1825, land and buildings in Utica- Genesee St.
Deed of above land from E.B. Sherman and wife Sarah to Robert Sherman
Lease from Thomas S. Taylor- Robert Sherman
Letter from Robert Sherman to Alexander Seymour and Sands Higinbotham

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: About Inventory of Olivia and Lucy Sherman from their executer of Watts Sherman Estate.

did.unittitle: Letter to Mrs. Robert Sherman (Maria) from Robert

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: About search for Jane Wilson and the meeting of friends.

did.unittitle: Will of Robert Sherman
Watts Sherman Estate Account
Letter to Joshua Hathaway, Esquire (Surrogate of County of Oneida from Robert Sherman)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Statement and report of the Olivia and Lucy Sherman estate.

did.unittitle: Letter to Robert Sherman from Maria Sherman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: About illness of Maria

did.unittitle: Letter to Mrs. Robert Sherman form Mr. H. Author

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: About Maria's illness and profession of faith in God.

did.unittitle: Letter to Mr. R. Sherman from B.B. Lansing

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Utica business.

did.unittitle: Letter to Lieut. Col. Talcott from Winfield Scott

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: subject: Mr. Benton's machine (invention) for throwing shots (balls)

did.unittitle: letter to Col. Talcott from Andrew Meneely

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "wants to sell levelling and surveying instruments to government--- asks Talcott to help him."

did.unittitle: Letter 2 invitations to Col. Talcott to visit the US ship "Princeton" at Greenleap Point(?) from Capt. R.F. Stockton.
Personal letters to Olivia Sherman Talcott from E.B. Sherman Utica
Letter to E.B. Sherman from Olivia Talcott "wants settlement papers of father's estate and advice on selling grandfathers farm to Cousin Joseph."
Account of R.S. estate sent to Olivia Talcott
Letter to S.V. Talcott Account of R.S. estate
Letter to Col. Talcott from James Baldwin

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Subject: school tax in district; money used to build a school.

did.unittitle: Letter to Col. Talcott from W.H. Sherman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Subject: administration of R.S. Estate also farm in Rhode Island.

did.unittitle: Estate R.S. to Estate E.B. Sherman.
Estate R.S. To estate W.H. Sherman.
Letter to S.V. Talcott from W.H. Sherman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Subject: selling farm in Rhode Island to cousin Joseph, investigation of funds paid by R.S. to Mrs. Holchetrass (?) a larger amount than entitled to receive.

did.unittitle: Letter to S.V. Talcott from Mr. Duncan

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Subject: value of farm in Rhode Island worth 300.

did.unittitle: Letter to S.V. Talcott from James Buchanan introducing Charles Kugler Esq.
Letter to S.V. Talcott from W.H. Seward

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Subject: introducing Babcock who has invented a new combination of metals.
